reasoning: In February 2025, Interim DC U.S. Attorney Ed Martin initiated 'Operation Whirlwind', an investigation into Senator Chuck Schumer over 2020 remarks, asserting it was about threats against DOGE employees. This widely covered incident strongly pushed toward a YES resolution. However, by early March, mainstream reporting confirmed that DOJ leadership (Main Justice) effectively blocked Martin from elevating the probe to a grand jury, leading him to drop 'Operation Whirlwind'. This reveals significant institutional resistance within the DOJ against prosecuting high-profile Democrats based on these theories, even under the Trump administration. The community prediction on May 1, 2025, stood at 0.50, strongly implying that the Schumer probe did not clearly satisfy the strict criteria for a 'DOJ announcement of a new investigation' in the minds of the admins or the forecasting community given the DOJ’s internal squashing of the effort, or at least that it remains hotly debated. Nevertheless, the fact that an attempt was made so early in the term (February) underscores the severe political pressure on the DOJ to pursue Trump's political opponents. With almost 8 months left until the 2026 deadline, Trump could replace defiant DOJ officials or appoint a Special Counsel who will carry out these promises. Given the ongoing tension between Trump’s retribution promises and the demonstrated institutional guardrails at Main Justice, the probability hovers around a coin flip.
